sessions.intent: Pressure-test the comparison redesign against the real branch code and write the settled architect plan.

before.assumptions: The coder's plan (.bot/compare-redesign/coder/compare-redesign-plan.md) framed compare as owned-per-type, async, no Type.Name switch. Read the real code to test the framing.
before.risk: Plan rules 1-2 (Value-is-raw, type-references-Data) and rule 8 (async because materialize is I/O) appeared to contradict the on-disk item.@this model; rule 4 (each type owns coercion) risked breaking antisymmetry.

actions.context: Verified the real comparison machinery on compare-redesign (two scout agents had read the recycled prevars-in-pr state by mistake). Confirmed: NormalizeTypes is symmetric; per-type values currently hold their backing (text holds string) and Data.Value mixes raw scalars and wrappers; the type entity already routes name->family behavior via Convert/Conversions; materialize is sync today.

Wrote the settled design after the whiteboard session with Ingi. Key corrections vs the coder plan: coercion by per-type rank (winner coerces loser) to preserve antisymmetry; one async value door await data.Value() with sync .Value removed; sync ordering core with sort two-phased to avoid sync-over-async; enum Incomparable is ordering-only with a null carve-out for equality; dispatch reuses the existing name->family routing rather than a new registry.

Locked the value-door details Ingi confirmed: public Value() returns ValueTask<object?> (sync-complete zero-alloc when present, async load only when pending) since it's the ~990-site hottest accessor; no public sync .Value property, just the private _value field; lazy I/O is the principle (path held until first touch). The sync framework methods split by consequence: GetHashCode/Equals/operators throw with guidance (wrong answer corrupts a dict; collections key on the raw materialised value not the wrapper), ToString degrades to '<text pending>' without I/O (debugger/logs render via ToString). Serialization materialises before the sync write boundary (already the codebase pattern). No #if DEBUG / GetAwaiter().GetResult() anywhere.

Addressed Ingi's review comment (5dd5618d25): rank was placed on Data (Data computed Rank(a)>=Rank(b) and reached into other.Type) — an OBP violation. Moved it onto the type: Data asks this.Type.Rank(other), passing the whole other Data (never other.Type), and the type returns the winner. Updated the dispatch code block, rule 7, the prose, and the You-own-this invariants. Marked the comment resolved via the review-server API.

Carved the plan into six stages plus the two test docs test-designer needs. Stage 1 enum, Stage 2 value door + value-as-raw flip (the big foundation, ~990 .Value reads), Stage 3 per-type rank/coerce/sync-order, Stage 4 Data.Compare, Stage 5 consumers + boundary mapping + two-phase sort, Stage 6 demolition + Diff rename. test-strategy.md narrates four integration cuts (cross-type antisymmetry, lazy read, sort-by-IO-key, enum boundary); test-coverage.md has the per-stage coverage matrix, the failure matrix, and the new-surfaces inventory. Two precision fixes made while writing and propagated back into plan.md: (1) enum NotEqual (reconciled-unequal-unordered, ordering errors) vs Incomparable (unreconcilable, all ops error) — the earlier 'Incomparable is ordering-only' was wrong given dict==number must error; (2) this.Type.Rank(other) returns the DRIVING TYPE and Order(a,b) compares in caller order, so no sign flip (ordering winner-vs-loser then flipping would be a latent bug).

Folded in coder v1 review (.bot/compare-redesign/coder/v1/comments.md), all six concerns verified against the branch and addressed: (1) async value source is net-new -> added Stage 2 Part A naming the shape + ILoadable fold; (2) ~990 overcounts and migration is per-receiver, not mechanical -> scoped to Data-receiver .Value only, views keep a sync .Value; (3) stages 2-4 are one green unit -> stated in plan index + stage deps; (4) GetHashCode/Equals throw must ship per-type with the raw-flip -> per-type sequencing note; (5) membership (contains/in/indexof/unique) treats Incomparable/NotEqual as no-match, never errors -> added membership column to Stage 1 boundary table + Stage 5 + test-coverage; (6) virtual Value override seam moves to the source/protected Load hook. Smaller: path default-compare-stays-sync explicit, ValueTask await-once wants an analyzer/grep gate.

PIVOT to the typed value model (type holds the value; data.Value() returns the typed value; raw CLR is the leaf exception via ToRaw). This reverses the abandoned draft rules 1-2 (raw CLR + views) and re-aligns with scalars-as-native, and is less work (no value-as-raw flip, no view construction; the value already owns compare/write). Deleted the six raw-model stage files and two raw-model test docs and rewrote plan.md as the typed-model spine. Rung model settled: one representation at a time (path -> binary/text -> dict), refine-and-replace, _raw dissolves into the binary/text rung, drop-on-refine (no double storage), verbatim passthrough = never-parsed path, display != parse. Carried over: Comparison enum + boundary, rank-on-type + caller-order, two-phase sort no-GetResult, membership-never-errors, Peek/Diff renames. Stages + test docs to be re-carved after Ingi approves the new spine.

Scope grew to the full typed value model (comparison is the first consumer). Settled: file + directory are new types in a reference-fundamental hierarchy (path -> file/directory -> image/audio/video); read X -> a file; write out %file% writes content (intent-based, file wire form = content, image precedent); text stays pure content (no .Path); no generic ToRaw (raw private, leaves only via the type Write/As<item>/gated per-type interop like path.Absolute, enforced by a build gate); As<T:item>; Pile 3 (full public-surface typing: path.Absolute->path, text.Length->number, every CLR-returning public member -> PLang type) is IN this branch as the final stage behind the gate. Rewrote plan.md as the typed-value-model spine with a 7-stage index (1-6 value model + comparison, 7 surface-typing last). Branch stays compare-redesign (rename deferred post-crash). Container crashed; restored from origin 6b1775c3c, nothing lost.

Updated the spine (plan.md only, per Ingi) with the navigation/serialization model settled this session: the meta-rule (the type decides — no central case taxonomy); the two access planes . (data) and ! (typed property surface = Pile 3), leading-! = implicit root, ! already in grammar; kinds are not values (json/csv are kinds+deserializers producing items; %x.type% field vs %x!type% PLang type); a reference (file/url/image) is stable with a lazy content facet (refine-and-replace describes only bare values) — corrects old rule 5; write out %x% is type-owned serialization (the type Write decides wire shape; directory serializes Entries recursively; no universal forward-to-!content); directory has Entries not content. No stages/test docs touched (Ingi: plan only).

Settled three open points into the spine. (1) References are stable and hold content (never replaced); the ! plane has two layers — own location props (!path/!scheme/!host, no materialise, intrinsic via path inheritance) and content props (!size, forward+materialise); no !source. (2) No bool-marker carve-out in the gate — IsTruthy returns @bool, engine-internal plumbing (IsLeaf) goes internal (out of the public-only gate) rather than exempted; only the gated interop accessor (path.Absolute) is exempt. (3) rank is a static per type for now. Open points trimmed to per-type carve details + two architect verifications (raw-CLR-bounded sampling, number boxing).

Carved 7 stage files + 2 test docs to the typed-value-model spine (for coder review). S1 enum, S2 typed value door + ./! resolver, S3 file/directory/url reference types (stable, two-layer !, dir.list:list<path>, type-owned serialization), S4 per-type Compare (static rank + coerce + enum), S5 data.Compare entry (async caller-order), S6 consumers + Pile-2 conversions + demolition (delete old mediator, Compare->Diff), S7 full surface typing + the gate. test-strategy = 5 integration cuts; test-coverage = per-stage matrix + failure matrix + new-surfaces inventory. Spine stage index links the files. Stages 2-6 are one green unit; S7 behind the gate.

after.status: Stages + test docs carved and pushed for coder review.
after.health: Complete handoff package on the typed-value-model spine.
after.notes: 7 stages + 2 test docs + linked spine. Awaiting coder read-over for gaps.

sessions.intent: Review the architect's comparison-redesign plan and stages, ground every load-bearing claim against the real branch code, and write peer comments for the architect.

before.assumptions: The plan describes one lazy ValueTask door, value-as-raw, rank-on-type, sync ordering core, and ~990 .Value reads to migrate. Read-only review — no implementation this session.
before.risk: Plan presents the lazy async source as existing infrastructure; the ~990 figure is a raw grep; stages 2-4 may not be independently green-able.

actions.context: Verified plan claims against code. Confirmed: Compare.Order returns raw int + throws NotOrderableException (the sign-bearing shape the enum replaces); NormalizeTypes symmetric; .Value count is exactly 990 but ~74 are non-Data (Lazy/KeyValuePair/Nullable) plus the type-wrapper views own a .Value accessor, so the grep conflates receivers. Found: no async _source exists — lazy is sync _valueFactory + _raw + sync Materialize, and ILoadable.LoadAsync is per-type (image/binary), not Data-level. Value is a virtual property with overrides (this.cs:1566). GetHashCode/Equals on views (TString, choice) are live keying surfaces.

Wrote six ranked concerns for the architect: (1) the lazy async I/O source is net-new and stage-sized, presented as one bullet — recommend splitting out; (2) the ~990 figure overcounts and mechanical migration is unsafe because views own .Value too — decide whether views keep a sync .Value; (3) stages 2-4 are not independently green-able, say so as a hard statement; (4) the GetHashCode/Equals throw must ship per-type with the raw flip or dict keying breaks mid-migration; (5) contains/in on an Incomparable element pair errors per the boundary table rather than returning not-found — pin the intended semantics; (6) Value is virtual with overrides, the method door loses that seam. Plus smaller notes (Peek/Diff renames clean, path default-compare-sync vs async drift, ValueTask await-once analyzer).

Architect's session block had malformed JSON (two stray array-close `]` after action objects that fell outside the actions array). Repaired to valid JSON, preserving all architect content, before appending this session.

after.status: Comments written to .bot/compare-redesign/coder/v1/comments.md and pushed. No code changed. Awaiting architect read.
after.health: Design sound; two stage-sized gaps flagged (async source, migration scope).
after.notes: Read-only review session. Verdict: build it; the spine is right. The two implementation-changing concerns are the net-new async source (#1) and the migration being non-mechanical / stages 2-4 not independently green (#2/#3).

sessions.intent: Review the architect re-carved typed-value-model plan + 7 stages against the real code; flag what is under-scoped or will not work.

before.assumptions: Spine pivoted to typed-value model since coder v1 (raw-CLR) review; stages re-carved.
before.risk: Re-carve may have dropped v1-won scoping (async source as its own stage).

actions.context: Grounded the re-carved typed-value-model plan against real code; 7 flags, 4 to fix pre-impl (async source de-staged, ! plane collision, path Content demolition, ToRaw in Data.Type).

after.health: plan is buildable; 4 stage-scoping fixes recommended before code
after.notes: No code written. Architect to fold flags.

sessions.intent: Review architect answers to coder v2 findings; verify the async-navigation safety claim against the code.

before.assumptions: All 7 v2 findings settled in stages.
before.risk: Async-navigation safety claim may have missed sync resolver/param-getter surfaces.

actions.context: Verified 6/7 answers solid. Found 1 substantive gap: async door collides with sync source-generated lazy param getters (cannot await) + sync Variable.Get/Resolve dotted navigation; read boundary must land off the sync surfaces. 2 secondary notes (in-place Type mutation aliasing; name-removal wire round-trip).

after.health: plan near-ready; one async-boundary question to pin before Stage 2
after.notes: No code. Build it once the read boundary is named.

sessions.intent: Review architect resolution of v3 findings A/B/C; verify the GetParameter<T>-lazy switch consequence.

before.risk: Lazy param getter may silently move the resolution-error guard timing.

actions.context: A/B/C resolved well. One mechanical consequence: GetParameter<T> is net-new; 42 handlers read param.Value!/.Success pre-await; lazy getter moves resolution-error guard to after await Param.Value() — silent regression if not reordered. Otherwise plan ready.

after.health: plan ready to implement once the guard-reorder note lands

sessions.intent: Author the test contract (C# TUnit + PLang .goal) for the typed-value-model branch — Stages 1-7 + the 5 integration cuts.

before.assumptions: Architect plan + coder v4 approval are settled (build it). The 2-6 unit lands as one green; Stage 7 rides behind a warning-then-error gate. Test docs at architect/plan/test-strategy.md + test-coverage.md are the contract source.
before.risk: Cross-axis tests (lazy door + narrow + chain-wide ! + param resolution-after-await) interact — picking the wrong layer per row gives weak coverage. Mitigation: follow the coverage matrix layer column strictly.

actions.context: Test plan with 7 batches, layer-mapping, folder layout.
125 C# TUnit stubs across 14 files, one per coverage-matrix row group. All Assert.Fail(Not implemented). Build clean.
15 PLang .test.goal stubs: 6 integration cuts + 5 failure-matrix + 4 plane/narrow surface.
Final test plan, verdict (pass=true), summary.

after.notes: 125 C# tests + 15 .goal tests. dotnet build PLang.Tests = 0 errors. Coverage maps to architect/plan/test-coverage.md. Next: coder.

sessions.intent: Implement the typed value model — Stage 1 enum first, then drive Stages 2-6 as one green unit.

before.assumptions: v1-v4 were reviews; no production code yet. Architect plan settled, test-designer stubbed ~140 tests. Baseline build clean.
before.risk: Stages 2-6 are one all-or-nothing green unit (async door rewrite + ~42 handler sites + source-gen); build stays red mid-flight.

actions.context: Stage 1 enum; green standalone.

Async door: ScalarValue->Peek(); Value property->ValueTask Value() method; Materialize() sync core (internal virtual; DynamicData override); ParseRaw() inner; SetValue() write; Data<T>.Value() typed async. OBP: sync read is the Materialize verb, NOT a CurrentValue/Materialized noun-twin (Ingi caught the verb+noun smell).
Generator emits Peek() for param diagnostics/presence guards, Materialize() for [Code] injection. 956 generated errors -> 0.

6 handler files migrated to async door: Run() async, params via await Param.Value(), guard-reorder (read.cs), Task.FromResult unwound, Materialize() for sync/build surfaces. Reference set for the recipe.

after.status: Stage 2 async-door cutover: FULL SOLUTION COMPILES (PLang+PlangConsole+PLang.Tests, 0/0/0). Production 2130->0 errors and 0 CS8974. Tests 1736->0 errors. KNOWN GAP: 130 CS8974 silent method-group .Value sites in TESTS (compile-clean but runtime-failing, ~8/95 DataTests). Stages 3-6 remain.
after.health: production green+clean; tests compile but 130 CS8974 cause some runtime failures (clean worklist: grep CS8974)
after.notes: Door=async ValueTask Value() + Materialize() sync core. CS8974 trap: .Value method-group->object is silent (not compile error, not TUnit-analyzer error). Recipe + worklist in summary.md.

sessions.intent: Resolve the verbose optional-param shape the Stage-2 .Value() migration produces; add a Stage-7 conversion discipline and carve a new Stage 8.

before.assumptions: Coder has landed Stage 2 (the async .Value() door + lazy GetParameter<T>). The migration mechanically produced `(Mime == null ? null : await Mime.Value()) ?? "text/plain"` at ~42 sites. Ingi flagged the verbosity.
before.risk: An optional param's nullable C# reference duplicates the absence Data already models via IsInitialized (OBP smell #6); under Nullable: enable a `?`-typed property dereference trips CS8602; the text->string crossing relies on an implicit operator Stage 7 removes.

actions.context: Verified the live model: data.@this<T>.Value is a sync property (this.cs:1489); the generator's Data-property emitter has four cases and only the nullable case returns C# null on IsEmpty (the source of `Mime == null`); [Default] already constructs a non-null default-carrying Data; Data already models absent via Uninitialized/NotFound (IsInitialized=false) vs the present-null sentinel; no == operator overload on Data; Nullable is enable in both csproj; text has implicit string<->text operators (text/this.cs:60-61) and public string Value (:48); TypeFromMime(string) keys a string ConcurrentDictionary (format/list:216,427).

Added the conversion discipline (deliverable + Design section): for any flagged site, change the receiving type to the PLang type, trace where it flows, and lower to raw CLR only where it's unavoidable - a .NET-library boundary (sqlite/Assembly.LoadFrom/HttpClient/Regex). Ingi's correction: our own C# is never a leaf - a string-keyed registry we wrote goes text-keyed (Dictionary<text,...> keys fine), not string-keyed-with-extraction. .ToString() forbidden as a value path. Worked example: channel.Mime string->text, traced into TypeFromMime(text) with the format registry itself going text-keyed; raw string only at the HTTP Content-Type handoff.

Made then reverted a `text.Value -> internal` edit. Ingi: coder has already shipped Stage 2 - hands off. The text.Value visibility question moves to Stage 7 where the gate flags it. Net: stage-2 unchanged.

Carved Stage 8: an optional param resolves to a non-null Data (Data.Uninitialized, never a C# null) so handlers read await Param.Value() / Value(fallback) - no == null, no !, no trailing ?? default. ? stays the optional signal (no [Optional]: forgetting ? -> required -> loud); generator stamps [System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNull] on ? slots so the deref doesn't trip CS8602 under Nullable: enable; [Default] carries static defaults (extended to fire on null too); .Value(fallback) for runtime defaults. Independent follow-on to Stage 2, not part of the 2-6 green unit. Flagged in the file + index as not-yet-coder-audited (coder to flag conflicts before implementing). One verify-API assumption to test first: [NotNull] on a partial property's implementing part honored by Roslyn for read-site flow analysis. Wired into plan.md index + summary.md table.

Ingi noticed the coder's finished Stage 2 uses Materialize() heavily, including sync methods that should be async. Confirmed the plan said Materialize() disappears (door is the single async accessor) but the coder kept it as a sync core (Value() => new(Materialize()), async read deferred to Stage 3) and calls .Materialize() directly at ~300 handler sites. Audited: 60 handler files (44 sync/the smell, 16 async), 87 name-slot reads, 227 value reads, 35 app/data internal. Risk is latent - today Value()==Materialize(), but Stage 3 puts async I/O read inside Value() so every direct .Materialize() bypasses it (count/where over file/url content never loads). Carved stage-2.1-materialize-to-door.md: rule (value reads -> await Value() + flip sync Run() async; name slots -> door; Data-internal/ToString/serialize -> leave as internal sync core), categorized worklist (A flip / B name-only / C swap / D leave / E review = navigation chain + two-phase sort + GoalCall), grep gate (app/module zero .Materialize()). Prerequisite for Stage 3. Wired into plan index + summary table.

Coder audited Stage 2.1 (coder/v5/stage-2.1-audit.md) - caught that it covered only one of three deferred async conversions. Verified all three claims against the branch: (1) GetChild is sync 'public virtual @this', NOT ValueTask - my bucket-E review-and-swap premise was factually wrong, nav-async is unbuilt; (2) GetParameter<T> doesn't exist + source-gen getter still emits eager As<T>(Context) (Emission/Property/Data/this.cs:40,44,54,58) - params resolve before any await; (3) navigators (app/variable/navigator/{List,Dictionary,Snapshot}.cs) read sync Materialize() and live in app/variable, which my gate missed. Honest picture: Stage 2 shipped async signature but sync substance. Rewrote stage-2.1 into 3 parts, all gating Stage 3: A handler value-reads -> await Value() (original scope); B navigation chain -> ValueTask (GetChild/navigators/Variable.Get + await-once gate + sync surfaces = v3 finding-A); C GetParameter<T> lazy getter + source-gen. Named A<->C coupling (await Param.Value() not lazy until C) and the Stage-8 overlap (both rewrite the same getter emission - do once). Fixed bucket E, widened gate to app/variable/navigator. Corrected Stage 2 status to 'async signature only' in summary table. Decision delegated by Ingi: one file with parts a/b/c, not three files.

after.status: Stage 2.1 expanded to 3 parts (A handler reads / B nav async / C param getter) after coder audit; pushed. Coder unblocked.
after.health: Stage 2 is async-signature-only; 2.1 (all 3 parts) is the real prereq for Stage 3. Stage 8 overlaps 2.1c (shared getter rewrite). Stage 8 still awaiting coder read-over.
after.notes: Coder's audit was correct on all three verified facts. Stage 2.1 now states the honest picture (signature vs substance). B-part sync surfaces (ToString/Equals/Fluid) and the await-once gate are the v3 finding-A resolution, designed never built. Two-phase sort stays Stage 6; getter emission shared with Stage 8.

actions_followup.context: Ingi: fold Stage 8 (optional-param null model) into Stage 2.1 Part C - same getter emission + same handler sites as 2.1c, so separate = rewrite getter twice + migrate optional-param sites twice (verbose then clean). Pushed back on his first thought (Stage 3): Stage 3 doesn't touch the getter emission, so the magnet is 2.1c, not 3. Expanded 2.1c to do lazy resolution + non-null Data.Uninitialized + [NotNull] stamp + [Default]-on-null + .Value(fallback) door overload in one rewrite; Part A migrates optional params straight to clean shape. Per Ingi, also told the coder to retrofit the v5-already-migrated handler files (variable/set, file/read, list/{contains,any,group,join}) to the clean shape - they predate the null model and look done. stage-8 file -> pointer stub; plan index marks it folded; summary table marks stage 8 folded. [NotNull]-on-partial-property is the verify-first check at the top of 2.1c.

Settled the two B+C decisions the coder asked in coder/v6/stage-2.1-bc-report.md. (1) Navigation async = Design-1 (ValueTask GetChild, async climbs Variable.Get/Resolve/As<T> + ~35 callers). I'd recommended the coder's Design-2 (sync GetChild + lazy child, async only at Value(), smaller blast radius, decouples B from C); showed Ingi both in code; he chose Design-1 - Design-2 hides async behind a sync-looking GetChild and has a write-back-identity trap (same instinct as no-exemptions: don't hide the reckoning). Added the flag: ripple must terminate, NO GetAwaiter().GetResult() - if it hits a sync wall, surface it (that's where D2's laziness would have hidden). Under D1, B + C + the ~200 method-group-.Value migration are one green-or-red unit. (2) Gate = NO exemptions (Ingi: proper language, no exceptions, will bite us). Dropped the allowlist; the genuinely-sync sites were using the wrong verb. Three verbs: await Value() (load), Peek() (in-memory, no I/O), Materialize() -> internal to Data so the compiler is the gate not grep. Serializer Write/GetHashCode/Equals/Fluid/--debug/build-meta -> Peek(); content reads -> await Value(); kill GetAwaiter().GetResult() in debug. Rewrote stage-2.1 (decisions note, Goal, Dependencies, per-site rule 3, Part B Design-1 + ripple-terminate, gate section, You-own-this).

sessions.intent: Implement Stage 2.1 — route handler reads/navigation/param-getter through the async Value() door (Part A first).

before.assumptions: Door is async signature only (Value()=>new(Materialize())); 272 Materialize in app/module; nav sync; getter eager. Production compiles 0 errors.
before.risk: B forces C via Variable.Get->As<T>; A separable. Keep production green.

after.status: Stage 2.1 Part A substantially done (app/module .Materialize 272->112, build green throughout). Remaining: 54 optional->C, ~44 gate exemptions (documented), ~4 Stage-6, ~10 scattered. Part B+C (nav async + getter rewrite + null model) NOT started — one all-at-once interlocked change with a design fork, unsafe to half-do AFK.
after.notes: Key finding: .Value(fallback) overload breaks ~200 method-group data.Value sites (ambiguity) — must land with C migration. B forces C forces the method-group migration. Design fork (ValueTask GetChild vs lazy-child) flagged for architect. Exemptions + B+C plan in v6/.

sessions.intent: Stages 4-6 closure (Pile-2) + Stage 3 reference types core

before.assumptions: Stages 4-6 green except Pile-2; Stage 3 unstarted
before.risk: read->reference inversion touches every read consumer

actions.context: Stage6 Pile-2: typed door / dict navigation / dict->json->STJ / slot accessor; no recursive ToRaw
Stage3 path demolition: _location backing, Content/Source removed, type-owned Write (portable form)
Stage3 reference types + renderers
Narrow-on-examination, scalar-content door, type chain Accumulate/Facet, family-name registry fix, consumers through the door
Stage3 contract updates (read->file headline) + stub fills; flagged to Ingi in summary

after.status: Stage 1-2.1,4-6 done; Stage 3 core green; stubs + Stage 7 remain
after.health: plang 317/0 (5 stale stubs), C# 0 real fails
after.notes: See coder/summary.md for the semantics line (scalar=content, examination=narrow) and test-contract flags

sessions.intent: Design session with Ingi producing data-value-model.md (the Data/Value target model); build/test speed investigation producing dev.sh + report

before.assumptions: Stage 9 consumer-patching was the right path
before.risk: patching consumers instead of fixing the model — confirmed wrong by Ingi; pivoted to design session

actions.context: The agreed Data/Value model from the design session: Data owns name/Type/properties; the type instance IS the value; async Value() rebinds on the type's answer; Peek vs Value; three rungs for C# types; type is an item; Assembly never in Data; schema layers replace nested Data; Materialize dies.
Reverted the half-done door retype to HEAD — disposable per the design doc; tree compiles again.
Measured build/test costs (dotnet run 90s+, analyzers 17s, after-idle stall 2min); dev.sh encodes fast workflow: 1-5s builds, 3s filtered tests, full gate before commit.

after.status: design doc pushed for architect review; speed workflow in place
after.health: tree compiles; C# suite ~5 known fails (stage9 tail, disposable path); plang suite green at last run
after.notes: Next: architect reads data-value-model.md; open items in doc (type-chain reconciliation, Value<T> vs As<T>); optional PLang.Tests split for 31s->5s test edits
